element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Early levels establish core traversal mechanics
Combat mechanics introduced alongside basic enemies
Weapon type varies per level; ammunition scattered throughout
Adds vertical traversal option to linear progression

element_introduction_order.element: Enemy variety (ghosts, piranhas, crabs, bulldogs, mummified mice)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Thematic enemies tied to TV world stages
Obstacles and hazards increase in complexity mid-game
Late-game shift from linear to exploratory pathfinding
Gate progression; defeated boss yields extra life opportunity

typical_relief_triggers: Commercial break with extra life opportunity
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Early levels maintain steady, approachable pacing with linear progression. Mid-game introduces increased obstacle complexity and varied enemy types. Late levels shift to exploratory maze-like design with higher spatial demands, building tension toward final confrontation with the Glitch boss.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Secrets and beneficial items hidden throughout large level spaces; player invited to explore populated stages
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Items and ammunition scattered as pickups; bonus round access between levels suggests hidden collection mechanics

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction to movement, jumping, and basic combat with straightforward linear progression
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased enemy variety, obstacle density, and environmental hazards; weapon type variation per level adds tactical adjustment requirements
difficulty_curve.late_game: Shift to maze-like exploratory design demanding route-finding; multiple paths to level end; sustained enemy pressure
notable_spikes: Transition from linear to maze-like exploration in later levels
Boss encounters at level ends serve as progression gates
Commercial break sequences between bosses offer risk-reward extra life attempts

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level represents different television world with thematic enemies (ghosts, piranhas, crabs, bulldogs, mummified mice); visual and mechanical themes tied to broadcast concept

Early levels present linear obstacle courses with straightforward paths. Later levels become increasingly maze-like, inviting multiple routes to reach boss encounter; player must explore to find optimal path
Each level culminates in boss fight. Defeat triggers commercial break intermission where player attempts extra life collection. Checkpoint system allows respawn at nearest progress marker upon defeat
Close-range weapons and thrown objects change between levels; ammunition scattered as collectibles. Forces tactical readjustment and encourages exploration to maintain offensive capability
